windows.embed_text: a wooden floor with a hole in it | a white square on a wood floor | a piece of paper on a wooden floor
windows.transcript: template inside the container and center those wicks with confidence. If you're working with a clear jar, you can place the template underneath the jar.
windows.old_description: A paper/cardboard wick guide held over the jar.
windows.embed_text_v2: centering candle wick using template, clear glass jar on butcher-block, wick stick across jar mouth, printed paper circle template under jar, wicking hack, top-down
windows.summary_v2: Demonstrating wick-centering hacks: using a template inside the jar, then placing a clear jar over a printed circle template.

seconds_v2.desc: Top-down view of a clear glass jar on a butcher-block wood surface with a round cardboard/wick-centering template seated inside its mouth.
A bare hand (yellow nails, floral wrist tattoo) reaches in and adjusts a wick through the hole of the centering template in the jar.
Hand sets a long wick stick across the jar mouth, centering the wick down through the template hole.
Hand lifts the now-empty clear glass jar off the surface (motion mid-move).
Blurred fast pan across the wood surface as the shot transitions to a white paper template.
Top-down of a white paper sheet with a drawn circle and a center dot lying on the wood surface (a printable centering template).
A bare hand lowers an empty clear glass jar onto the paper, aligning its rim with the drawn circle to center the wick.
The clear jar sits centered over the template circle/dot; a tool is brought in at the bottom edge to adjust the wick.
